prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model. We assume an annualized drift (expected return) of 9% and an annualized volatility of 20% for GS, projected over 20 trading days. The point estimate represents the median expected price, and the confidence intervals are derived from the log-normal distribution of prices inherent in the GBM model, reflecting the estimated volatility over the prediction period. This method inherently assumes that future price movements will reflect historical average trends and volatility, which may not hold true in reality. Stock market predictions are subject to significant uncertainty.
